verdict_reasoning: SRC is a canonical cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase with no intrinsic membrane anchor. However, it exhibits documented surface display in cancer contexts — tumor cells show elevated Src kinase activity and surface-localized pools driving oncogenic signaling. Additionally, Src undergoes myristoylation and can transiently associate with the PM during signaling and cell activation. The protein has a documented non-baseline surface pool in transformed / activated cells via membrane recruitment and lipid binding, qualifying as cell-state-induced surfacing.
